2012-08-01.title: Blackout Continues in India
2012-08-01.abstract: The power failure spread across 22 of the country’s 28 states, an area whose population is nearly 700 million, almost 10 percent of the world’s population.

2012-08-01.title: With Brazil as Advocate, Venezuela Joins Trade Bloc
2012-08-01.abstract: After a long diplomatic struggle, the nation joined Mercosur’s four full members, including Brazil, which was influential in the admission decision.

2012-08-01.title: Pakistan’s New Spy Chief Visits Washington at a Time of Frayed Relations
2012-08-01.abstract: Little is known about Lt. Gen. Zahir ul-Islam, the new head of Pakistan’s powerful Inter-Services Intelligence directorate, an agency vital to United States interests in the region.

2012-08-01.title: Uncertainty Over Letter Underscores Delicate Ties
2012-08-01.abstract: Egyptian and Israeli officials contradicted each other’s accounts of how or even whether their two presidents had communicated.

2012-08-01.title: White House and Congress Are in Step Over Iran Sanctions
2012-08-01.abstract: New measures intensify existing efforts to choke off Iran’s oil and shipping revenue. Officials said the measures were not tied to Mitt Romney’s suggestion of a tougher line on Iran.

2012-08-02.title: Catholic Church and University in Peru Fight Over Name
2012-08-02.abstract: The Pontifical Catholic University has refused to change its name or to enact other changes that would give the church more control over its operations.

2012-08-02.title: Afghan Finance Minister Under Scrutiny
2012-08-02.abstract: Omar Zakhilwal, Afghanistan’s finance minister, has come under scrutiny for bank records that suggest he accepted sizable payments from businesses and individuals and transferred money to Canada.

2012-08-02.title: Mexico: Former Defense Officials Are Accused of Aiding Traffickers
2012-08-02.abstract: The officers, who were initially detained in May, are accused of participating in trafficking with the Beltrán Leyva gang, which had been one of the biggest suppliers of cocaine to the United States.

2012-08-02.title: Teddy Bears Fall From Sky, and Heads Roll in Minsk
2012-08-02.abstract: Belarus’s president, Aleksandr G. Lukashenko, reacted sharply to a security breach in which Swedish pilots airdropped 800 stuffed bears bearing anti-authoritarian signs on Minsk.

2012-08-02.title: Egypt Releases Partial List of Ministers
2012-08-02.abstract: The choices by Prime Minister Hesham Qandil included some holdovers from the military-backed team but few Islamist ministers.

2012-08-03.title: Russia Pulls the Plug on Drunken Paratroopers’ Swimming Pool of Choice
2012-08-03.abstract: To prevent drownings, Russian cities drained public fountains on the soldiers’ national holiday.

2012-08-03.title: John Keegan, Historian Who Put a Face on War, Dies at 78
2012-08-03.abstract: Mr. Keegan was an Englishman widely considered to be the pre-eminent military historian of his era and the author of more than 20 books, including the masterwork “The Face of Battle.”

2012-08-03.title: Syrian Leader’s Arms Under Strain as Conflict Continues
2012-08-03.abstract: Many of the Syrian government’s most powerful weapons are looking less potent and in some cases like a liability for the military of President Bashar al-Assad.

2012-08-03.title: After Warnings of an Olympic Crush, Businesses Suffer in a Deserted London
2012-08-03.abstract: Fears of the Gridlock Games have transformed into complaints about the Ghost Town Olympics as some tourists skip London, bowing to official warnings of stifling overcrowding.

2012-08-03.title: Where Europe Trails Asia: The Airport Experience
2012-08-03.abstract: For the average traveler passing through Munich and Beijing, which airport feels more like the product of an authoritarian society?

2012-08-04.title: For Rebel Fight in Syrian City, Baking Bread Is Urgent Task
2012-08-04.abstract: Bread is a mainstay of the Syrian diet, eaten with every meal, and in Aleppo, paralyzed by two weeks of war, the bakery lines show that basic commerce has become a battleground.

2012-08-04.title: Beach Essentials in China: Flip-Flops, a Towel and a Ski Mask
2012-08-04.abstract: In China, where pale skin is a sign of status and solar protection something of an obsession, stretchy ski masks are catching on as beachwear.

2012-08-04.title: U.S. Begins Packing Its Afghan War Gear for the Movers
2012-08-04.abstract: As the Afghan war draws to a close, the American military has already began an immense logistical effort, spanning half the globe, to ship back its equipment.

2012-08-04.title: Pakistan: Contempt Law Rejected
2012-08-04.abstract: Chief Justice Iftikhar Muhammad Chaudhry called the law, which provided greater protection to the prime minister and other senior government officials against such charges, unconstitutional and contrary to the independence of the judiciary.

2012-08-04.title: China: Muslim Fasting Discouraged
2012-08-04.abstract: Several local governments in the western region of Xinjiang have ordered Muslim restaurants to stay open during the holy month of Ramadan and are telling civil servants and students to continue to eat and drink during daylight hours.

2012-08-05.title: Florence Waren, Jewish Dancer Who Resisted Nazis, Dies at 95
2012-08-05.abstract: In occupied France, Ms. Waren, who danced as Nazis sat in her audience, hid her identity and helped to hide other Jews and to smuggle weapons to the resistance.

2012-08-05.title: Uzbek Group Says Drone Killed Leader
2012-08-05.abstract: The news will bolster the Obama administration’s claims that the drone program led by the Central Intelligence Agency is successfully degrading Al Qaeda and its allies.

2012-08-05.title: Leader’s Torture in the ’70s Stirs Ghosts in Brazil
2012-08-05.abstract: A Brazilian truth commission is gathering details on decades-old torture cases, including that of President Dilma Rousseff.

2012-08-05.title: A Call to Investigate Russian Governor’s Comments
2012-08-05.abstract: In announcing the Cossacks would be paid to maintain public order, Governor Aleksandr Tkachev of the Krasnodar region, site of the 2014 Olympics, stressed the need to control the migration of darker-skinned Muslims from the North Caucasus.

2012-08-05.title: An Olympian Effort: A Chinese Dictionary
2012-08-05.abstract: Summer Olympic sports carry their own particular vocabularies, and they seem to bring out the quadrennial linguist in all of us. In China, the official dictionary of Mandarin is also gettin’ jiggy with some jargon of its own, from “house slaves” and “banana person” to “diplomagate” and “NBA.”

2012-08-06.title: Militias Eager to Oust Islamists in Mali
2012-08-06.abstract: The recruits have almost no weapons, little military instruction and not much more than the hard ground to sleep on.

2012-08-06.title: Afghan President Moves to Reassure Allies After Security Ministers Are Dismissed
2012-08-06.abstract: President Hamid Karzai said he respected Parliament’s decision to fire two senior security officials, but it was not clear how quickly he would replace them.

2012-08-06.title: Writer Defends His Handling of Interview With Assad
2012-08-06.abstract: Jürgen Todenhöfer, a critic of conflict, defended his interview with Syria’s leader and spoke of his motivation to hear what his subject had to say.

2012-08-06.title: Saying Mali ‘Is Our Country,’ Militias Train to Oust Islamists
2012-08-06.abstract: Fledgling citizen militias have taken on the task of standing up to an array of Islamist fighters bent on enforcing a hard-edged brand of Shariah law in Mali.

2012-08-06.title: A Coming-Out Party in Hanoi
2012-08-06.abstract: A gay pride parade Sunday in Hanoi was a first for Vietnam’s socially conservative capital. Gay-rights advocates have been buoyed by the news that the National Assembly is likely to consider a proposal to legalize same-sex marriage.

2012-08-07.title: Where Streets Are Thronged With Strays Baring Fangs
2012-08-07.abstract: Free-roaming dogs in India, many of which are rabid, number in the tens of millions and bite millions of people annually, including vast numbers of children.

2012-08-07.title: A Snarling Menace in India
2012-08-07.abstract: Tens of millions of free-roaming dogs — and the rabies they carry — are wreaking havoc across the country.

2012-08-07.title: Cracks in British Governing Coalition as House of Lords Overhaul Falls Apart
2012-08-07.abstract: The proposed changes would have nearly cut in half the size of the upper chamber, from 826 to 450 members, and made 80 percent of the body elective by 2025.

2012-08-07.title: In Germany, Talk of the Olympics Centers on a Rower’s Choice of Boyfriend
2012-08-07.abstract: Nadja Drygalla’s relationship with a man known for his extreme right-wing political activities had made her Olympics story a cautionary tale.

2012-08-07.title: Occupy’s Frankfurt Camp Is Closed as Health Hazard
2012-08-07.abstract: The encampment outside a bank had been set up in October and was tolerated by the police until Monday, when they dismantled tents after complaints of a rat infestation.

2012-08-08.title: Party Bristles at Military’s Push for More Sway in China
2012-08-08.abstract: With China’s once-a-decade leadership transition nearing, Communist Party leaders are pushing back against what some see as military officials’ intrusion into policy and politics.

2012-08-08.title: Heavy Burden on Athletes Takes Joy Away From China’s Olympic Success
2012-08-08.abstract: Many of China’s athletes are sent as adolescents to live at state-financed sports academies, where the training is grueling, and they do not see their families for years.

2012-08-08.title: In Syria, Potential Threat to Government Air Power Emerges
2012-08-08.abstract: For the first time since the conflict in Syria began last year, an activist opposed to President Bashar al-Assad has publicly presented possible evidence that the antigovernment forces have obtained a heat-seeking, shoulder-fired anti-aircraft missile.

2012-08-08.title: Primer: Brazil’s Rise to Global Player
2012-08-08.abstract: Simon Romero, the Rio de Janeiro bureau chief for The New York Times, discusses what led to Brazil’s growth, despite the economic downturn, and the challenges the country faces.

2012-08-08.title: Brazil Gains Business and Influence as It Offers Aid and Loans in Africa
2012-08-08.abstract: Brazil, which has more people of African descent than any other country outside of the continent itself, is raising its profile there again by building on ties from the Portuguese empire.

2012-08-09.title: For Victim in Sikh Temple Shooting, a Life of Separation
2012-08-09.abstract: A stunned family that had lived for so long without its father and husband is now preparing to travel to Wisconsin to collect Ranjit Singh’s body.

2012-08-09.title: U.S. and Gulf Allies Pursue a Missile Shield Against Iranian Attack
2012-08-09.abstract: Such a regional defense system is meant to send a pointed message to Tehran, but it will require partner nations to put aside historic rivalries, share information and coordinate their arsenals.

2012-08-09.title: North Korea: Is That a Dior, Comrade?
2012-08-09.abstract: Ri Sol-ju, the wife of Kim Jong-un, was pictured with what appeared to be a Christian Dior handbag. The bag’s going price in Seoul is $1,600, about 16 times the monthly wage of some of the country’s best-paid workers.

2012-08-09.title: China: Typhoon Leads to Evacuation
2012-08-09.abstract: Landing near Shanghai, a typhoon forced more than a million people were forced to evacuate and the city’s two major airports delayed or canceled hundreds of flights.

2012-08-09.title: Mexico: Attack Kills Four in Acapulco
2012-08-09.abstract: The attack appeared to be the work of organized criminals, according to an official from the state prosecutor’s office.

2012-08-10.title: Russia: Activist Fined Under New Law
2012-08-10.abstract: A Moscow court on Thursday ordered Eduard Limonov, a political opposition leader, to pay a $380 fine for organizing an unsanctioned demonstration.

2012-08-10.title: Mexico: Drug Suspect Extradited to U.S.
2012-08-10.abstract: Sandra Ávila Beltrán, the first woman to be charged as the leader of an international drug-smuggling operation, faces cocaine-smuggling charges filed in a United States District Court in Florida.

2012-08-10.title: Britain: Judge Is Picked to Lead Inquest Into Death of Former K.G.B. Officer
2012-08-10.abstract: The hearings to be overseen by Sir Robert Owen could provide the first opportunity for witnesses to testify under oath regarding the death of Alexander V. Litvinenko.

2012-08-10.title: Videos Shed Light on Chaos at Fukushima as a Nuclear Crisis Unfolded
2012-08-10.abstract: The released videos offer the first minute-by-minute account of the last-ditch effort to avert what would become the worst nuclear calamity since Chernobyl.

2012-08-10.title: Cleaning Agent Orange
2012-08-10.abstract: Over a decade of war in Vietnam, the United States sprayed about 20 million gallons of Agent Orange, a defoliant linked to cancers, birth defects and other diseases.

2012-08-11.title: After Sinai Killings, Cairo Tightens Grip on a Neglected Region
2012-08-11.abstract: The northern Sinai Peninsula has seen a buildup of troops and military hardware since a fatal attack Sunday on Egyptian soldiers who were about to break their Ramadan fast.

2012-08-11.title: U.S. Accuses Hezbollah of Aiding Syria’s Crackdown
2012-08-11.abstract: The United States said the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah has trained and advised government forces inside Syria and has helped to expel opposition fighters from within the country.

2012-08-11.title: Strategy for Syrian Refugees in Turkey Is on Clinton Agenda
2012-08-11.abstract: Secretary of State Hillary Rodham Clinton arrives in Istanbul on Saturday, and one topic on the table is the question of a safe haven for Syrian rebels near the Turkish border.

2012-08-11.title: Colonel in Cover-up Case to Be Tried in Mexico Civilian Court
2012-08-11.abstract: Rights groups say the Mexico Supreme Court’s ruling against Col. Jose Guadalupe Arias Agredana in the death of Jethro Sanchez could upend the way the nation deals with abuses by the military.

2012-08-11.title: Japan Sales Tax Increase Passed, on Pledge of Early Election
2012-08-11.abstract: The Japanese Parliament approved Prime Minister Yoshihiko Noda’s plan to double the sales tax and address national debt, but only after he made a promise to hold elections “soon.”

2012-08-12.title: South Korean Denied Medal Over Politics
2012-08-12.abstract: South Korean soccer player Park Jong-woo was barred from receiving his bronze medal on Saturday for displaying a sign with a political message after his team’s victory in the third-place game.

2012-08-12.title: For Asia, So Far, Paul Ryan Remains Out of Focus
2012-08-12.abstract: In Beijing, Tokyo, Seoul and other major capitals across Asia, the early reaction to Representative Paul Ryan’s selection as Mitt Romney’s running mate was muted. One commentator said Mr. Ryan “ticks most boxes” for the Republican candidate, while another said he might restrain Mr. Romney’s “potentially disastrous China-bashing.”

2012-08-12.title: Earthquakes in Iran Kill Hundreds
2012-08-12.abstract: At least 250 people were killed Saturday when two powerful earthquakes struck several towns near the Iranian city of Tabriz.

2012-08-12.title: London 2012: A Gold Medal, or An Also-Ran?
2012-08-12.abstract: Drugs and drama, world records, an eccentric opening, historic breakthroughs, women boxers, a man with no legs. Quite an Olympics. What thrilled you? Made you weepy? What was rubbish? Let us know.

2012-08-12.title: Silver and Bronze Are Not Equal
2012-08-12.abstract: Equality in the sports arena means an equal opportunity and a level playing field. But ultimately, sports are competitive and the pecking order should be reflected on the podium.

2012-08-13.title: Will Paul Ryan Make a Difference in the U.S. Election?
2012-08-13.abstract: Looking at a host of historical precedents, the predictors are not very auspicious for Paul Ryan’s chances of becoming vice president of the United States.

2012-08-13.title: Key Officials Under Karzai Are Criticized
2012-08-13.abstract: The Afghan cabinet ministries that work most closely with Western countries are under intense pressure from Parliament and an anti-graft commission.

2012-08-13.title: Korea Policing the Net. Twist? It’s South Korea.
2012-08-13.abstract: As one of the world’s most wired societies, South Korea has embraced the Web, but a recent crackdown on Internet freedom has drawn accusations of censorship.

2012-08-13.title: Effort to Cut Milan Traffic Halted as Court Favors Garage
2012-08-13.abstract: A $6 fee that helped reduce driving in the city center was eliminated with a ruling that upheld the appeal of a parking garage that had lost business because of the measure.

2012-08-13.title: Traffic Jams Aggravate Around the World
2012-08-13.abstract: Milan has entered a legal battle over traffic control. But gridlock is a global problem. Here is a collection of curated videos showing snarls, backups and standstills around the world.

2012-08-14.title: Trying to Protect a Reef With an Otherworldly Diversion
2012-08-14.abstract: Nearly 500 statues in an underwater museum in Cancún, Mexico, serve as part of a conservation effort of the Mesoamerican Reef, the second-largest barrier reef system in the world.

2012-08-14.title: Britain Basks in a Golden Afterglow
2012-08-14.abstract: Contrary to all the grumbling that preceded them, the Olympics offered a finer sense of a Britain relaunched.

2012-08-14.title: Turkmenistan: Convicts Pardoned
2012-08-14.abstract: President Gurbanguly Berdymukhammedov has ordered the release of 1,327 convicts to observe the end of the Muslim holy month of Ramadan.

2012-08-14.title: Egypt Lifts a Junior Corps Impatient Over Military Failure
2012-08-14.abstract: In his purge of Egypt’s top generals, President Mohamed Morsi leaned on the support of a junior officer corps that blamed the old guard for a litany of problems within the military.

2012-08-14.title: Palestinian Seeks Diplomatic Allies
2012-08-14.abstract: Hanan Ashrawi, a member of the Palestine Liberation Organization’s executive committee, asked diplomats on Monday to support a renewed bid for observer-state status at the United Nations.

2012-08-15.title: China: Self-Immolations Continue
2012-08-15.abstract: Two young Tibetan men set themselves on fire on Monday in a Tibetan region of western China to protest Chinese rule.

2012-08-15.title: Mexico: Criminal Leader Found Dead
2012-08-15.abstract: The leader of a criminal syndicate operating in the volatile region of Chéran, in the state of Michoacán, was found slain on Tuesday, according to the state attorney’s office.

2012-08-15.title: U.S. Vows to Speed Aid to Iran Earthquake Victims
2012-08-15.abstract: American sanctions make charitable giving to Iran slow and complicated, but the Obama administration has promised to speed applications for donations to help victims of recent earthquakes.

2012-08-15.title: Ecuador: President Denies Decision to Grant WikiLeaks Founder Asylum
2012-08-15.abstract: The president of Ecuador, Rafael Correa, denied on Tuesday a report on the Web site of The Guardian that he had decided to grant asylum to the WikiLeaks founder, Julian Assange.

2012-08-15.title: Grandmas Grow Gold in Swaziland
2012-08-15.abstract: Many of the marijuana-farming grandmothers of Swaziland, who supply South Africa’s drug market, started growing to support the offspring their children left behind when they died.

2012-08-16.title: Mexico: Confessions Are Reported in the Killing of 5 Journalists
2012-08-16.abstract: The Mexican authorities announced Wednesday that captured members of a criminal cell had confessed to the killing of five journalists in the state of Veracruz this year.

2012-08-16.title: Iran Group May Remain on U.S. Terror List
2012-08-16.abstract: The Mujahedeen Khalq, also known as M.E.K., has been lobbying to be removed from the State Department’s terrorist list, but its refusal to leave a camp in Iraq has caused concern.

2012-08-16.title: Syrian Rebels Said to Be Holding Elite Fighters From Iran
2012-08-16.abstract: American officials said they had evidence that hostages taken by Syrian rebels were members of Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps, not pilgrims as described by the government in Tehran.

2012-08-16.title: Israeli Leaders Could Be Dissuaded From Striking Iran
2012-08-16.abstract: Statements by a former Israeli national security adviser seemed to buttress a theory that Israeli saber rattling is primarily an effort to push the United States to bolster sanctions.

2012-08-16.title: Egypt’s Islamist Leaders Accused of Stifling Media
2012-08-16.abstract: Facing strident criticism, President Mohamed Morsi’s government has suspended a satellite television channel, confiscated copies of a newspaper and censored columnists in a state newspaper.

2012-08-17.title: Svetozar Gligoric, Who Pioneered Chess Moves, Dies at 89
2012-08-17.abstract: Mr. Gligoric was considered one of the greatest players of the 20th century and pioneered several systems of moves, but he never won the chance to compete for the world championship.

2012-08-17.title: A Society’s Dizzying Changes Cast the Police in the Role of Relationship Counselor
2012-08-17.abstract: As societal changes transform Bangalore, many young Indians are struggling to cope, and they take their problems to the only symbol of authority that is accessible around the clock: the police.

2012-08-17.title: Murder Arrests in Mexico Met With Skepticism
2012-08-17.abstract: The suspects in the killings of three photojournalists and a publicist were picked up on drug and weapons charges, and confessed to the murders.

2012-08-17.title: Assange Faces Long Stay in Ecuador’s London Embassy
2012-08-17.abstract: The word the WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange has used more than any other is free, but he now finds himself in a genteel prison, without limit of sentence.

2012-08-17.title: Canada: Police Find Human Head in an Ontario River
2012-08-17.abstract: The Peel Regional Police were unable to immediately confirm whether the remains came from one person.

2012-08-18.title: Protester Briefly Skitters Out of Russian Jurisdiction
2012-08-18.abstract: A woman called for the release of three women from a punk rock band, then led the police on a chase to Turkish Embassy grounds before returning to Russian soil.

2012-08-18.title: Watching the Olympics, Seeing Formula One
2012-08-18.abstract: Formula One was fortunate to find that its annual August break of five weeks between races coincided with the greatest sporting circus on earth, at the Olympics in London. While the racing series hibernated, the Olympics nevertheless gave time – and a reason – for Formula One fans and journalists to brainstorm about their sporting series by comparison.

2012-08-18.title: Thailand: 4 Die in Nightclub Fire in Phuket
2012-08-18.abstract: The fire occurred after power was cut and then restored during heavy rain in the early hours of Friday, Phuket’s chief of police said.

2012-08-18.title: Push for More Cholera Inoculations
2012-08-18.abstract: Pressure to adopt a cholera vaccine as part of the routine response to outbreaks mounted as two expert panels advised the World Health Organization to use it.

2012-08-18.title: A Gaza Border Slams Shut, and With It, Chances for a Pilgrimage to Mecca
2012-08-18.abstract: After an attack that killed 16 Egyptian soldiers, a crossing was closed, then severely restricted, preventing many Gazans from going to Mecca during Ramadan.

2012-08-19.title: Do Argentines Need Therapy? Pull Up a Couch
2012-08-19.abstract: The number of practicing psychologists in Argentina has been surging, making the country a world leader, at least when it comes to people’s broad willingness to bare their souls.

2012-08-19.title: Afghan Attacks on Allied Troops Prompt NATO to Shift Policy
2012-08-19.abstract: The military’s efforts to stop “insider attacks” are indications of how destabilizing the deaths of coalition troops at the hands of Afghan forces have become.

2012-08-19.title: Support Grows in Germany for Vote on Giving Up Power to European Bloc
2012-08-19.abstract: Political leaders increasingly support allowing Germans to have a direct say in whether to give up more power to European Union institutions.

2012-08-19.title: A Firing-Squad Verdict in Chinese Murder Case?
2012-08-19.abstract: It’s certainly not your plain-vanilla murder trial. Gu Kailai, the wife of one of China’s most powerful (and now deposed) political bosses, has reportedly confessed to the murder of a British businessman. A guilty verdict is widely expected — a decision is scheduled for Monday — but what about her sentence? Should she face a firing squad? Or something more creative?

2012-08-19.title: The Perseverance of the Long-Distance Swimmer
2012-08-19.abstract: Diana Nyad, who turned 63 on Wednesday, has ended her fourth attempt to swim from Cuba to Florida. Still, she personifies the global athlete, inspiring people around the world to stay fit — and to continue to achieve new goals — at all stages in life.

2012-08-20.title: In Dieppe, Remembering the Fallen
2012-08-20.abstract: More than 2,000 people descended on Dieppe, France, on Sunday to commemorate one of the bloodiest days of World War II.

2012-08-20.title: A Day for Canada’s Fallen in a Lesser-Known Battle
2012-08-20.abstract: The Dieppe raid, the first amphibious attack on German-occupied France by the Allied forces during World War II, remains one of the most deadly conflicts in Canadian history.

2012-08-20.title: China Defers Death Penalty for Disgraced Official’s Wife
2012-08-20.abstract: Gu Kailai, the wife of Bo Xilai, received a suspended death sentence for the poisoning death of a British business associate. In the Chinese legal system, such a sentence is often commuted to life in prison.

2012-08-20.title: Simmering Chinese Anger at Japan Is Now on the Boil
2012-08-20.abstract: Street protests and bellicose talk in China are increasing tensions between Beijing and Tokyo in their dispute over some barren, rocky islets. Chinese protesters smashed Japanese cars and attacked sushi restaurants. A Chinese general wants to mine the islands and use them for bombing practice.

2012-08-20.title: Sustainable Innovation: Reducing Fashion’s Carbon Footprint
2012-08-20.abstract: Hope that an unglamorous machine could revolutionize what becomes of our designer threads — and the rest of our clothes — once they’re passé.

2012-08-21.title: Christian Girl’s Blasphemy Arrest Incites a Furor in Pakistan
2012-08-21.abstract: A girl was jailed after being accused of burning a religious textbook used to teach the Koran to children.

2012-08-21.title: In China, Gu Kailai’s Reprieve Reinforces Cynicism
2012-08-21.abstract: The prosecution of the wife of the purged political leader Bo Xilai brought questions about official corruption and political favoritism within the Communist Party into the open.

2012-08-21.title: Obama Threatens Force Against Syria
2012-08-21.abstract: President Obama warned Syria on Monday that it would face American military intervention if there were signs that its arsenal of unconventional weapons was being moved or prepared for use.

2012-08-21.title: The Lions of Tawhid
2012-08-21.abstract: The Times’s C.J. Chivers travels with an antigovernment fighting group in and near Aleppo, where the war for Syria’s future has hardened all involved.

2012-08-21.title: With the Rebels in the Battle for Aleppo
2012-08-21.abstract: A group of Syrian rebels seeks to destroy the government and its better-equipped forces by most any means. Their collective confidence that they will prevail both bonds them together and informs their sense that this is their time.

2012-08-22.title: Beneath a Humdrum Exterior, Talk of Mysterious Inner Depths
2012-08-22.abstract: A book about the campaign of President François Hollande of France says that behind his bland facade is a man of self-mastery and doggedness, with a mysterious core.

2012-08-22.title: Risks of Syrian Intervention Limit Options for U.S.
2012-08-22.abstract: The options for military action remain limited by what administration officials have described as a simple calculus: it would make the conflict worse.

2012-08-22.title: Dispute Over Islands Reflects Japanese Fear of China’s Rise
2012-08-22.abstract: A chain of islands at the center of a territorial dispute has Japanese nationalists pushing their country to boldly counter the explosive growth of China and South Korea.

2012-08-22.title: Skinny-Dipping in Israel Casts Unwanted Spotlight on Congressional Travel
2012-08-22.abstract: Reports of a skinny-dipping lawmaker in Israel last year have put unwanted attention on the practice of private groups paying for foreign travel.

2012-08-22.title: Progress Is Cited on New Reactor in North Korea
2012-08-22.abstract: North Korea has been making significant progress on a nuclear reactor widely seen as a cover for making more weapons, analysts said.

2012-08-23.title: Developments in Iran and Sinai Deepen Israel’s Worries About Egypt
2012-08-23.abstract: With Egypt’s deployment of tanks in the Sinai Peninsula and its president’s upcoming trip to Iran, Israeli officials are increasingly worried about what has long been a critical regional relationship.

2012-08-23.title: Egypt Requests $4.8 Billion From I.M.F.
2012-08-23.abstract: The Egyptian government has requested a $4.8 billion loan from the International Monetary Fund in its latest attempt to secure financing for the country’s badly damaged economy.

2012-08-23.title: Less Support for Opposition in Zimbabwe, Study Shows
2012-08-23.abstract: A new survey of Zimbabweans suggests that support for the opposition party there has fallen while support for President Robert Mugabe’s party has increased.

2012-08-23.title: A Conversation With: Assam Students’ Union Adviser Bhattacharya
2012-08-23.abstract: Illegal immigration, porous borders are root causes of the current problems in Assam, adviser says.

2012-08-23.title: The Fatal Shore, Awash in Plastic
2012-08-23.abstract: On Midway Atoll — 3,200 miles from both Seattle and Seoul — the scourge of plastic pollution in the oceans is obvious and disturbing. Need proof? Just look inside a dead albatross. The American artist Chris Jordan has.

2012-08-24.title: China Pressed to Prevent  Abuse of Psychiatric Confinement
2012-08-24.abstract: While there is virtually no public debate in China on the fate of dissidents in the country’s psychiatric system, involuntary commitment of non-dissidents by family members and others has become a hot topic.

2012-08-24.title: Australia Increases Refugee Quota
2012-08-24.abstract: Prime Minister Julia Gillard ordered an immediate increase on Thursday in the number of refugees that Australia accepts yearly.

2012-08-24.title: Attacks on Afghan Troops by Colleagues Are Rising, Allies Say
2012-08-24.abstract: Afghan soldiers or police officers have killed 53 of their comrades and wounded at least 22 others in 35 separate attacks this year, according to NATO data.

2012-08-24.title: Export Powerhouse Feels Pangs of Labor Strife
2012-08-24.abstract: As Bangladesh garment workers have seen their meager earnings eroded by double-digit inflation, protests and violent clashes with the police have become increasingly common.

2012-08-24.title: Egyptian President’s Move Ends Detention of Critic
2012-08-24.abstract: President Mohamed Morsi outlawed pretrial detention of one of his critics in an early test of his ability to break with the past.

2012-08-25.title: A Rapper and Poet Pushes for a New French Identity of Inclusion
2012-08-25.abstract: Abd Al Malik, a Catholic altar boy turned Muslim proselyte, uses lyricism to try to come to terms with the ethnic and religious diversity of France.

2012-08-25.title: 2 U.S. Employees Wounded in Ambush on Mexican Road
2012-08-25.abstract: Two Americans were shot and wounded on Friday when the United States Embassy vehicle they were driving was ambushed by a caravan of cars that included Mexican police.

2012-08-25.title: Tropical Storm Isaac Lashes Haiti, Still Crippled by Earthquake
2012-08-25.abstract: Tropical Storm Isaac unleashed heavy rain over Haiti on Friday, raising the threat of floods and mudslides that could worsen the misery of a nation still devastated from the 2010 earthquake.

2012-08-25.title: Syrian Forces Strike Again in Suburbs of Damascus
2012-08-25.abstract: Syrian troops continued to pound the suburbs of Damascus on Friday, as fighting raged in Aleppo and rebels in the eastern city of Deir al-Zour reported seizing a contested armory.

2012-08-25.title: Murdoch Paper Defies a Warning and Exposes Prince Harry
2012-08-25.abstract: The scandal-hungry Sun tabloid on Friday defied a warning from British officials and published images Prince Harry cavorting naked during a game of strip billiards in his Las Vegas hotel suite.

2012-08-26.title: Brazil’s Ex-President Is Back on Front Lines
2012-08-26.abstract: Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, the former president of Brazil, is winning over onetime rivals for his allies, and is anything but shy when it comes to blasting Europe’s handling of its debt crisis.

2012-08-26.title: Chinese Deny Forcing Refugees to Myanmar
2012-08-26.abstract: The Chinese foreign ministry says fighting in Myanmar is subsiding, contradicting other reports.

2012-08-26.title: Down to Earth, But Heroic Nonetheless
2012-08-26.abstract: The word hero is not used very much these days, not in a serious way, although it certainly applied to the astronaut Neil Armstrong. It might also apply to six Asians being honored this week for their civic efforts, which, while certainly more terrestrial than Mr. Armstrong’s lunar accomplishments, do have something of the heroic about them.

2012-08-26.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Editions
2012-08-26.abstract: The roots of Assam violence, how the Mumbai police mishandled the Azad Maidan protests and a lost girl returns from the forest decades later.

2012-08-26.title: Crackdown Toll Seen as Syrians Bury Hundreds
2012-08-26.abstract: With more than 200 bodies found over the weekend, the assault in Daraya, a suburb of Damascus has begun to look like one of the deadliest short-term raids of the uprising.

2012-08-27.title: At Summit Meeting, Iran Has a Message for the World
2012-08-27.abstract: As Iran plays host to the Nonaligned Movement meeting, it wants to tell its side of the long standoff with the Western powers over its suspect nuclear program.

2012-08-27.title: Fires Still Burning in Aftermath of Deadly Venezuela Blast
2012-08-27.abstract: Firefighters struggled to control blazes near the Amuay oil refinery where an explosion killed at least 39 people on Saturday.

2012-08-27.title: Arming the World
2012-08-27.abstract: Last year, the United States surged far ahead of other countries as a weapons supplier, particularly to the developing world.

2012-08-27.title: Freedom Rock? Not In China
2012-08-27.abstract: Two members of the Russian punk group Pussy Riot are on the run, fearful of arrest for their “punk prayer” against President Vladimir Putin. Young Chinese musicians, despite frustrations with their own government, have not often ventured into such overtly political territory, in part because of a deeply felt patriotism.

2012-08-27.title: How Hate Gets Counted
2012-08-27.abstract: It is wrong to assume that every attack against a Sikh in America is really meant for a Muslim, the authors say.

2012-08-28.title: China: 2 Tibetan Teenagers Set Themselves on Fire
2012-08-28.abstract: Two Tibetan teenagers set themselves on fire and burned to death on Monday in an anti-Chinese political protest in a Tibetan-populated area of western China’s Sichuan Province.

2012-08-28.title: In Mexico, Rehearsing to Inject Drama Into the Courtroom
2012-08-28.abstract: As part of the overhaul of its justice system, Mexico is seeking to move away from a system where judges rule on court cases behind closed doors, based on written evidence.

2012-08-28.title: For Putin, Report Says, State Perks Pile High
2012-08-28.abstract: In a new report, Russian opposition leaders describe the presidential perks enjoyed by Vladimir V. Putin, including palaces, a fleet of jets and droves of luxury cars.

2012-08-28.title: Gangs’ Truce Buys El Salvador a Tenuous Peace
2012-08-28.abstract: A truce between two notoriously violent street gangs endures in El Salvador after five months, but some wonder whether the government has made a deal with the devil.

2012-08-28.title: After Attacks, Israeli Schools Confront Hate
2012-08-28.abstract: As some two million Israeli children started school on Monday, a national conversation also began over an attempted lynching on Aug. 16 that left an Arab teenager near death.

2012-08-29.title: Victims’ Sons in Tough Fight for Redress After China Rail Crash
2012-08-29.abstract: Two Chinese-American brothers’ odyssey to retrieve their parents’ bodies and gain compensation for a deadly high-speed railroad crash highlighted the workings of an unbending government agency in China.

2012-08-29.title: Malcolm W. Browne, Pulitzer-Prize Winning Reporter, Dies at 81
2012-08-29.abstract: Mr. Browne, who took one of the most memorable photos of the Vietnam War, won a Pulitzer Prize in 1964 for his reporting.

2012-08-29.title: Libya Officials Seem Helpless as Sufi Shrines Are Vandalized
2012-08-29.abstract: The country’s interior minister said he lacked the means to stop well-armed extremists from destroying sacred Sufi sites, adding he was willing to see all shrines destroyed to avoid bloodshed.

2012-08-29.title: The Last Journey
2012-08-29.abstract: The sons of two victims of China’s first high-speed-train crash prepare to bring their parents’ bodies home to New York. The family is locked in compensation talks with Chinese authorities.

2012-08-29.title: Americans Shot in Mexico Were C.I.A. Operatives Aiding in Drug War
2012-08-29.abstract: The two Americans wounded last week when their embassy vehicle was shot up were Central Intelligence Agency employees aiding in Mexico’s efforts to fight drug traffickers, officials said.

2012-08-30.title: Reginald Bartholomew, Busy Diplomat, Dies at 76
2012-08-30.abstract: Mr. Bartholomew, a senior American diplomat, negotiated for nuclear disarmament with the Soviet Union and survived a bomb attack while he was ambassador to Lebanon in 1984.

2012-08-30.title: Germany: Old Bomb Is Detonated
2012-08-30.abstract: An intentional detonation of a 550-pound World War II bomb found in Munich set off fires late Tuesday that damaged a shop and nearby apartment buildings.

2012-08-30.title: Britain: Former Journalist at Murdoch Paper Arrested for Hacking
2012-08-30.abstract: A former journalist with Rupert Murdoch’s Times of London newspaper was arrested Wednesday on suspicion of computer hacking during his time there, Scotland Yard said in a statement.

2012-08-30.title: Kenya: Officers Hurt by Grenade
2012-08-30.abstract: Seven Kenyan police officers were wounded, some critically, late Wednesday in the second grenade attack in two days in the port city of Mombasa, the police said.

2012-08-30.title: Book on Bin Laden Killing Contradicts U.S. Account
2012-08-30.abstract: A new firsthand account of the Navy SEALs raid that killed Osama bin Laden raises questions about whether the leader of Al Qaeda posed a clear threat to the commandos who fired on him.

2012-08-31.title: Hopes Raised, Roma in France Still Face a Date With the Bulldozer
2012-08-31.abstract: Foreign Roma in France said President François Hollande’s promises had led them to believe that their lot would improve, but so far his approach has been much like that of his predecessor.

2012-08-31.title: Selected Excerpts of Morsi’s Speech
2012-08-31.abstract: Excerpts from the speech by President Mohamed Morsi of Egypt at the meeting of the Nonaligned Movement in Tehran on Thursday, Aug. 30, 2012, as translated by The New York Times.

2012-08-31.title: Brazil Enacts Affirmative Action Law for Universities
2012-08-31.abstract: The law, signed Wednesday by President Dilma Rousseff, seeks to reverse the racial and income inequality that has long characterized Brazil.

2012-08-31.title: No Charges Filed on Harsh Tactics Used by the C.I.A.
2012-08-31.abstract: Attorney General Eric H. Holder Jr.’s announcement on Thursday eliminates the last possibility that any criminal charges will result from brutal interrogations carried out by the C.I.A.

2012-08-31.title: Report on Iran Nuclear Work Puts Israel in a Box
2012-08-31.abstract: An International Atomic Energy Agency report detailing Iran’s ramped up nuclear capabilities may force Israel to strike Iran or concede it cannot act on its own, according to experts.
